definition: The Dollar Smile posits three FX regimes: risk-off dollar strength (left), weak-growth dollar weakness (middle), and strong-growth dollar strength (right). It is descriptively useful but analytically limited because its three regimes exhaustively cover all macro states, making it unfalsifiable as typically deployed. Should be treated as a scenario-organizing heuristic, not a predictive model.
evidence: Historical counterexamples: 2017-18 US outperformed but dollar weakened 10%; Q2-Q3 2020 risk-off but dollar weakened after initial spike
No agent produced a real-time regime identification rule with documented hit rates
Three regimes exhaustively cover all macro states, making the framework unfalsifiable
rates_strategist_02 recalibration (DXY 95-108) critiqued as not following from evidence
Pair_3 synthesis: poor explanatory model but useful scenario-organizing tool if users commit to ex-ante regime identification
open_questions: Whether any regime identification rule can achieve >60% hit rate on 1-year dollar direction
How fiscal dominance may bifurcate the left tail (anti-dollar risk-off)
Whether the fiscal dollar smile / asymmetric smirk concept meaningfully recalibrates the framework
